title: Flask App — Incident Response Overview
description: 4 Golden Signals + business metrics + host infrastructure + PostgreSQL + SLO error budget.

panels.title: Service Health
panels.description: Live now. Turns red the moment Prometheus can no longer scrape the Flask app.

panels.title: Error Rate %
panels.description: 5xx error rate over last 5 min. Populates once Bronze wires http_requests_total.

panels.title: p95 Latency
panels.description: 95th percentile request duration. Populates once Bronze wires http_request_duration_seconds.

panels.title: CPU %
panels.description: Host CPU utilisation from node_exporter. Live now.

panels.title: DB Connections
panels.description: Live Postgres connections from postgres_exporter. Red at 20 = pool exhaustion.

panels.title: Latency — Distribution Heatmap
panels.description: Latency bucket distribution over time. Brighter = more requests in that duration band. Shows distribution shifts instantly during incidents. Populates once Bronze wires http_request_duration_seconds.

panels.title: Traffic — Requests per Second
panels.description: Incoming request rate by endpoint. Use the Endpoint dropdown to filter to a single route.

panels.title: Errors — 5xx Rate
panels.description: Fraction of requests returning 5xx. Background turns red at 5%. Populates once Bronze wires http_requests_total.

panels.title: Saturation — Active In-Flight Requests
panels.description: Current number of requests being processed simultaneously. Populates once Bronze wires active_requests.

panels.title: URL Shortener — Redirect Hit Rate
panels.description: Fraction of redirects that resolve vs 404. Populates once Bronze wires url_shortener_redirects_total.

panels.title: Saturation — DB Pool Connections
panels.description: Active Postgres connections via app pool. Populates once Bronze wires db_pool_connections_active.

panels.title: Traffic — Request Rate by Status Code
panels.description: 2xx vs 4xx vs 5xx breakdown over time. Populates once Bronze wires http_requests_total.

panels.title: CPU Usage %
panels.description: Host CPU utilisation. Sustained > 80% is a saturation signal.

panels.title: Memory Usage %
panels.description: Host memory utilisation. Approaching 100% may cause OOM kills.

panels.title: Disk I/O
panels.description: Disk read/write throughput. A spike here during slow DB queries confirms I/O bottleneck.

panels.title: Network I/O
panels.description: Network throughput in/out. Distinguishes CPU-bound vs network-bound slowness.

panels.title: DB — Active Connections
panels.description: Live connections to hackathon_db from postgres_exporter. Red at 20 = pool exhaustion.

panels.title: DB — Transaction Rate
panels.description: Commits and rollbacks per second. A drop during high traffic = DB bottleneck.

panels.title: DB — Deadlocks / min
panels.description: Deadlocks are always a bug. Any non-zero value here warrants investigation.

panels.title: Error Budget Remaining
panels.description: SLO: 99.5% success rate. 100% = full budget, 0% = SLO breached. Populates once Bronze is wired.

panels.title: SLO Compliance Over Time
panels.description: Error budget burn rate. > 1.0 means consuming budget faster than the SLO allows.

panels.title: App Uptime
panels.description: How long the Flask app has been continuously up.

panels.title: Total Requests
panels.description: All-time request count processed by the app.

panels.title: Latency — P50 / P95 / P99
panels.description: All three percentile lines on one graph. P99 spiking while P50 is flat = tail latency issue.

panels.title: Traffic by Endpoint
panels.description: Share of total requests per endpoint.

panels.title: Endpoint Activity Timeline
panels.description: Green = active traffic, red = silent. Shows which endpoints went quiet during incidents.

panels.title: Top Endpoints
panels.description: Req/s and p95 latency per endpoint.

panels.title: Application Logs
panels.description: Live structured log stream from Loki. Color-coded by level.

panels.title: Error & Load Simulation
panels.description: Generate errors, traffic spikes, and concurrent user load against the running app. Watch the panels above react in real time.

panels.title: Live Error Rate (watch during simulation)
panels.description: 5xx error fraction — watch this spike when you fire error bursts.

panels.title: Live Request Rate (watch during simulation)
panels.description: Requests per second — watch this spike when you run traffic spikes or concurrent users.

options.content:
<style>
@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Orbitron:wght@400;600;700&family=Share+Tech+Mono&display=swap');

/* Rounded panels with cyan border glow */
.react-grid-item > div:first-child,
[class*='panel-container'],
.panel-container {
  border-radius: 14px !important;
  overflow: hidden !important;
  border: 1px solid rgba(0, 188, 212, 0.45) !important;
  box-shadow: 0 0 28px rgba(0, 188, 212, 0.25), 0 0 8px rgba(0, 188, 212, 0.2), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(0, 188, 212, 0.12) !important;
}

/* Orbitron for panel titles */
[class*='panel-title'] h6,
[class*='panel-title'] span,
[class*='title-text'] {
  font-family: 'Orbitron', sans-serif !important;
  font-size: 10px !important;
  font-weight: 600 !important;
  letter-spacing: 0.1em !important;
  text-transform: uppercase !important;
  color: rgba(0, 229, 255, 0.85) !important;
}

/* Share Tech Mono for all values and body text */
body,
[class*='stat-text'],
[class*='stat-value'],
[class*='graph-legend'],
[class*='gauge'],
svg text,
.grafana-app {
  font-family: 'Share Tech Mono', monospace !important;
}
</style>
